construct.label: Economic and vocational anxiety
construct.intended_use: Four-item development pool for measuring anxiety about AI reducing occupational skill value, compensation, bargaining power, status, and retraining security.

development_pilot.sampling_frame: United States adults aged 18 or older eligible for the 2026 wave 1 calibration survey.
development_pilot.recruitment_source: Probability-aligned online panel or documented nonprobability panel with preregistered quota and weighting variables.
development_pilot.fielding_dates: Planned in preregistration before outcome inspection
development_pilot.exclusion_summary: No responses have been collected; exclusions will follow the preregistered attention, timing, comprehension, duplicate, and eligibility rules.
development_pilot.provenance_notes: This development-only dossier reserves the calibration evidence record for the economic/vocational construct pool before fielding.

confirmation_sample.sampling_frame: Independent United States adult respondent pool with no respondent overlap with the development pilot.
confirmation_sample.recruitment_source: Separate panel batch or probability-aligned sample source documented before confirmation analysis.
confirmation_sample.exclusion_summary: No confirmation responses have been collected; exclusions will be applied before CFA, reliability, IRT, DIF, and invariance analysis.
confirmation_sample.provenance_notes: The confirmation sample is required before any construct or domain scoring approval can be granted.

refresh_or_bridge_sample.sampling_frame: No released scored item version exists yet, so no bridge sample is required for this initial development pool.
refresh_or_bridge_sample.recruitment_source: Not applicable until item wording, language, administration mode, or scoring is revised after release.
refresh_or_bridge_sample.fielding_dates: Not applicable for v0.1.0 development-only status
refresh_or_bridge_sample.exclusion_summary: No bridge exclusions are applicable because no bridge sample exists.
refresh_or_bridge_sample.provenance_notes: A future bridge dossier is required before claiming comparability across revised item versions or administration modes.

efa.method: Ordinal exploratory factor analysis using polychoric correlations and oblique rotation unless the preregistration justifies a different estimator.
efa.decision_rule: Retain only items with interpretable primary loadings, acceptable cross-loading behavior, adequate response spread, and coverage of the economic/vocational anxiety construct.
efa.result_summary: No development pilot responses have been collected; EFA evidence is pending and cannot support scoring.
efa.release_consequence: All four items remain development_only and excluded from official scoring until EFA evidence is completed and reviewed.

cfa.method: Confirmatory factor analysis of the retained economic/vocational model in an independent sample using an estimator suitable for ordered categorical responses.
cfa.decision_rule: Approve construct scoring only if the retained model satisfies the protocol fit thresholds or a preregistered justification explains limited use.
cfa.result_summary: No independent confirmation sample has been fielded; CFA fit and loading evidence are unavailable.
cfa.release_consequence: Construct, domain, overall, longitudinal, and event-study scoring are not permitted for this item pool.

reliability.method: Ordinal omega or coefficient alpha with corrected item-total correlations and sensitivity to removing each retained item.
reliability.decision_rule: Require reliability at or above the protocol threshold for early scored construct use and no item that materially degrades the retained construct score.
reliability.result_summary: No reliability estimates exist because calibration responses have not yet been collected.
reliability.release_consequence: The dossier cannot support an internally reliable construct score in v0.1.0.

irt.method: Graded response model or documented ordinal alternative estimating discrimination, category thresholds, item information, and local dependence.
irt.decision_rule: Retain items only if thresholds are ordered or substantively justified, discrimination is useful, and local dependence does not duplicate construct coverage.
irt.result_summary: No IRT calibration has been run; item parameters are unavailable.
irt.release_consequence: No IRT-based scoring, linking, or information claims are permitted for this development pool.

dif.method: Ordinal logistic DIF screening, multi-group IRT, or documented equivalent for preregistered demographic, occupational, and AI-exposure comparison groups.
dif.decision_rule: Block scored comparison use for any item with statistically supported and practically meaningful unresolved DIF in an intended public comparison group.
dif.result_summary: No DIF results exist because the confirmation sample has not been analyzed.
dif.release_consequence: The item pool cannot support subgroup comparisons, weights-based comparisons, or event-study heterogeneity claims.

invariance.method: Multi-group CFA, alignment, IRT linking, or bridge design matched to the planned comparison claim.
invariance.decision_rule: Permit latent mean, longitudinal, or cross-group comparisons only when scalar invariance or a preregistered validated linking alternative is supported.
invariance.result_summary: No invariance analysis has been conducted for v0.1.0.
invariance.release_consequence: The dossier permits only development administration and prohibits benchmark comparison claims.

retention_table.observed_result: Covers perceived erosion of core software engineering skill value.
retention_table.threshold: Candidate pool must span multiple economic and vocational threat mechanisms.
retention_table.rationale: The item is substantively relevant but lacks EFA, CFA, reliability, IRT, DIF, and invariance evidence required for retention.

retention_table.observed_result: Covers wage and bargaining-power pressure from customer-support automation.
retention_table.threshold: Candidate pool must span multiple economic and vocational threat mechanisms.
retention_table.rationale: The item is substantively relevant but lacks calibration evidence required for construct scoring or public comparison use.

retention_table.observed_result: Covers forced retraining and occupational transition pressure in accounting work.
retention_table.threshold: Candidate pool must span multiple economic and vocational threat mechanisms.
retention_table.rationale: The item remains a development candidate until preregistered pilot and confirmation evidence justify retention.

retention_table.observed_result: Covers perceived occupational status and prestige loss in creative work.
retention_table.threshold: Candidate pool must span multiple economic and vocational threat mechanisms.
retention_table.rationale: The item has substantive construct coverage but no psychometric evidence sufficient for scored release.

decision.approval_scope: Development administration only; no official ANX scoring, longitudinal comparison, or event-study outcome use.
decision.rationale: The dossier identifies the four-item economic/vocational construct pool and planned validation evidence, but no calibration sample has been fielded and no psychometric approval evidence exists.

reviewer_signoff.notes: This development-only dossier is schema-valid for release tracking but does not constitute approval for scored benchmark use.
